name: WmiEvent - WmiEventConsumer activity detected
definition: Sysmon Event ID 20 records WMI event consumer registration activity.
trigger_scenarios: Sysmon emits this event when a WMI event consumer is registered. Microsoft documents that the event records the consumer name, log, and destination.

key_fields.explanation: The WMI consumer name recorded by Sysmon. Correlate it with the Event 21 consumer path to prove a complete subscription.
The destination recorded for the consumer. Command execution destinations that reference powershell.exe, cmd.exe, or C:\\Users\\ paths are high-risk because the consumer can execute attacker content.
The log field recorded by Sysmon for the consumer and useful for identifying scripted, command-line, or log-backed consumers.

false_positives: Approved administration, management agents, and deployment tools can generate this telemetry.
Baseline expected hosts, signed binaries, and change windows before suppression.

attck_mapping.technique_name: Event Triggered Execution: Windows Management Instrumentation Event Subscription

detection_notes: Alert when Destination references powershell.exe, cmd.exe, wscript.exe, mshta.exe, or a payload under C:\\Users\\, C:\\ProgramData\\, or C:\\Windows\\Temp. Microsoft documents Event 20 as recording consumer name, log, and destination, and MITRE T1546.003 covers WMI consumers used to execute code from event subscriptions. Correlate the consumer Name with Event 19 filter registration and Event 21 binding to determine whether the executable destination is armed for persistence.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 20
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Image, ProcessGuid
spl_snippet: index=sysmon EventCode=20 | table _time, host, Image, ProcessGuid
sample_log:
UtcTime: 2026-07-13 01:00:00.000
Name: UpdaterConsumer
Log: CommandLineEventConsumer
Destination: powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File C:\\ProgramData\\updater.ps1

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 20
queries.query:
Event
| where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 20
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is collected into Event.
Map raw XML fields to the documented fields.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 20
queries.query:
index=sysmon source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=20
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Add the documented event-specific conditions after field validation.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 20
assumptions: Confirm the Sysmon provider because event IDs are not globally unique.
Inspect raw fields because parsed names vary.

queries.title: Sysmon Event ID 20
queries.query:
title: Sysmon Event ID 20
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: sysmon
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 20
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.

value_references.meaning: A WMI event consumer was registered.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret EventID with the full event and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: The consumer destination that executes or handles the triggered event.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret Destination with the full event and correlated activity; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Sysmon Event ID 20 mean?
faqs.answer: A WMI event consumer was registered.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 20 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Enterprise management software can install legitimate consumers.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 20 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Review destination and name and correlate the consumer with Events 19 and 21 to reconstruct the subscription.

sources.title: Event Triggered Execution: Windows Management Instrumentation Event Subscription (T1546.003)
